blurb:
If you've never done Brazilian all-you-can-eat barbecue, this is a good place to try it without the intimidation factor of the fancy places in Buckhead. It's relaxed and family-friendly, perfect for celebrating something or just treating yourself to a meat-heavy dinner. They also serve breakfast and have lighter options.

The barbecue keeps coming to your table until you flip your card to red, and the cheese bread is addictive. The feijoada stew is authentic and hearty if you want to try something traditional. Fair warning that you'll probably eat more than you planned.

Go hungry and pace yourself. The salad bar has good options to balance out all the meat, and the staff is good about explaining how everything works if it's your first time.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sun 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
This place gets packed for a reason. The tacos are legit, the drinks are strong, and the vibe is always buzzing. Good for girls' night, casual dates, or when you want Mexican food that's a step up from your usual chain spot.

The esquites are addictive and perfect for sharing while you decide what else to order. The pitcher drinks like the pineapple guava are dangerously good and meant for groups. The tacos have creative combinations but nothing too weird for picky eaters.

It stays open until midnight most nights, which is clutch when you're out late and want real food. Just know it can get loud when it's busy, so maybe not the best choice for quiet conversation.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is where we take out-of-town visitors when we want to show off a little. The patio sits right on the Chattahoochee River, and watching the sunset over the water while eating dinner feels pretty special. Perfect for anniversaries, business dinners, or when your parents are visiting and want somewhere nice.

The seafood is what keeps us coming back. The salmon is consistently good, and they do Southern-style preparations that feel fancy but not weird. The portions are reasonable for the price point, and the service actually knows what they're doing.

Make a reservation, especially for weekend dinners or if you want a patio table. Parking can be tight when they're busy, but valet is available if you don't mind paying for it.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
This is my go-to spot when I want really good Indian food without the fancy price tag. It's in a strip mall near Perimeter Mall, and the inside is colorful and casual with big tables perfect for sharing. Great for family dinners or when you want to introduce friends to authentic Indian flavors.

The biryani is what they're known for, and it's legit. The portions are huge and come family-style, so bring your appetite or plan to take leftovers home. My family always orders way too much because everything looks so good, but that's half the fun.

Just know they get busy on weekends, especially Saturday evenings. The service can be a little slow when it's packed, but the food is worth the wait.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA

blurb:
This is one of those places that makes you feel like you discovered something special. The Persian food is authentic and flavorful, and the atmosphere works for everything from girls' night to date night. It's become one of my regular spots for trying something different.

Start with the hummus and definitely get the chicken shish kabobs. The rice with lentils and raisins sounds weird but totally works. The cocktails are creative and well-made, not just an afterthought. Save room for the tiramisu because it's worth it.

They break between lunch and dinner, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to eat. Make a reservation for weekend dinners because word is getting out about this place.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is where I go for real French pastries and the kind of breakfast that feels special without being stuffy. The owner is French and you can tell the difference in the baking. Perfect for weekend brunch with friends or grabbing fresh bread and pastries to take home. It gets busy but moves efficiently.

The quiches are excellent and change based on what's in season. The baguettes are as close to Paris as you'll get in Marietta, and the petit fours are perfect for bringing to parties. Their brunch menu hits the right balance of French technique and approachable flavors.

Get there early on weekends because the popular items sell out. They close at 6pm during the week and 3:30 on Sundays, so don't plan on late afternoon visits.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 3:30 PM

blurb:
This is Marietta's answer to those late-night cravings or early morning meetings before anyone else is open. The neon sign has been a landmark for decades, and being open 24/7 means it's saved me more times than I can count. Perfect for post-game meals with the kids or when you need breakfast at 2am.

The menu is huge and includes Greek specialties alongside classic diner fare. I always go for the big breakfast platters because the portions are generous and the eggs are done right. My kids love the pancakes, which are legitimately fluffy and not from a mix.

Just know it gets busy during peak hours, especially weekend mornings. But the service moves fast and there's usually a table within 15 minutes.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
This is perfect when your group can't agree on what to eat because there are twenty different vendors under one roof. It's right on the square, so you can walk around downtown after eating. Good for families with picky kids or meeting friends who all want different things.

The variety is impressive and the quality is better than your typical food court. Each vendor focuses on their specialty, so you're getting pizza from pizza people and tacos from taco people. There's everything from Asian to barbecue to vegetarian options.

Seating can get tight during peak hours, especially weekend lunch. But you can also grab food and eat outside on the square when the weather's nice.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sun 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
This is where I go when I want Middle Eastern food that's both authentic and won't break the bank. It's casual and family-friendly, perfect for introducing kids to new flavors or grabbing a quick healthy lunch. The staff is patient with questions if you're not familiar with the menu.

The shawarma is what most people order and it's excellent. The falafel is crispy outside and fluffy inside, and they have good veggie options if you're trying to eat lighter. Everything comes with their homemade pita bread that's way better than what you get at the grocery store.

Parking can be a little tight during lunch rush, but it's worth it. They also have baklava and other desserts if you want to grab something sweet to take home.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
My kids discovered this place and now it's our regular Friday pizza spot. The thin-crust style is authentic Italian, not the thick American stuff, and they use really good ingredients. It's casual enough for families but nice enough for date night too.

The margherita pizza is simple but perfect, and they do some creative combinations that actually work. The salads are surprisingly good if you want something lighter to share. The crust has that perfect crispy-chewy thing going on that makes you want another slice.

They can get busy on weekend evenings, but turnover is pretty quick since it's not a linger-over-wine kind of place. Worth the short wait when there is one.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
